summary: Tested Alyssa Driver Checkpoint v2.0 (driver-facing). Backend 18/18 pytest passed (init idempotent, name, SOP, 6 initial photos w/ auto-cair-T1, daily w/ 409 dedup, daily delete, BASTK x6 cap, resi, cair gates, static file serving). Frontend full flow verified at mobile 414w: URL param ingest, realtime clock ticking, NoPol banner, name save -> greet, SOP modal w/ 10 items + mark read, 6 photo slots all visible w/ proper data-testid, cair card shows Rp 1.750.000/1.050.000/700.000/150.000, handover card with BASTK + Resi buttons. Dark theme premium look is clean and consistent.

ui_bugs.issue: Driver name renders with a leading '.' (e.g. '.Budi Driver') due to literal '.' character before {trip.nama_driver} in JSX

action_items: Fix typo in /app/frontend/src/DriverCheckpoint.jsx line ~302: change '>.{trip.nama_driver}<' to '>{trip.nama_driver}<' (remove the stray leading dot in the greeting).
critical_code_review_comments: DriverCheckpoint.jsx is ~620 lines; consider splitting TahapCard + SOP modal + sections into smaller files for maintainability.

Backend uses today_wib() (WIB) but frontend computes todayStr from browser local date — if user is in a non-WIB timezone, frontend 'todayDone' calculation can disagree with backend gating. Backend is the source of truth, but UI may briefly mislabel state. Suggest using server-returned today date.
Server still imports ConfigDict from pydantic but never uses it; minor cleanup.
No max file-size enforcement on backend uploads (frontend caps 8MB/15MB). Backend should also enforce to be safe.
init_trip ignores additional fields if existing doc found (correct - idempotent), but does not validate that requestor's payload matches stored values; acceptable for current use case.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all major flows pass; one cosmetic typo)

test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created TRIP-TEST-* docs in MongoDB via pytest (with uploads under /app/backend/uploads/TRIP-TEST-*). Frontend test created TRIP-FE-TEST-001.
context_for_next_testing_agent: API is v2.0 (driver checkpoint, not GPS dashboard). Use the pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_driver_checkpoint.py as regression. Trip flow: init -> driver-name -> sop-read -> 6x initial (auto-cair T1) -> daily (409 same day) -> bastk (max 6) -> resi -> cair T3. Frontend renders at '/' and requires URL params trip + nopol. Mobile viewport 414w recommended.
rca of the issue:
Greet leading-dot bug RCA: In DriverCheckpoint.jsx around line 302, JSX literal is '
.{trip.nama_driver}
' — the period before the curly brace is rendered verbatim. Remove the '.' to fix. Reproduction: any non-empty nama_driver causes the prefix dot to appear in the header greet.
